description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that builds a Docker image for publishing using the variables CONTEXT (.), DOCKERFILE (docker/Dockerfile.publish) and IMAGE_TAG (${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}-publish, inherited from a .build template). Add a job named publish:image:creation that extends the .build template to run docker build -f $DOCKERFILE -t $IMAGE_TAG $CONTEXT and docker push $IMAGE_TAG. Add a second job named publish:image:test in a custom stage test-publish that sets CONTAINER_NAME to rafaeldutra-me.published and executes: docker container run --name $CONTAINER_NAME --publish 8080:8080 --detach $IMAGE_TAG, then docker container run --rm curlimages/curl:7.74.0 $(docker inspect $CONTAINER_NAME -f '{{range .NetworkSettings.Networks}}{{.IPAddress}}{{end}}'):8080 to verify the service. Use the globally defined variables CONTEXT, DOCKERFILE and IMAGE_TAG, with no caches, artifacts or special triggers.
